title: Mustardy Silky Slovakian Grain Pilaf
description: A comforting, sautéed grain dish from Slovakia, with a tangy twist from mustard and a silky texture from the addition of olive oil. Perfect for a weeknight dinner, this recipe is quick and easy to make, and can be adjusted to suit your dietary preferences.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the diced onion and cook for 5 minutes, or until softened.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, or until fragrant.
Add the quinoa, jasmine rice, and buckwheat groats, and stir to coat with oil and mix with onion and garlic.
Add the grainy mustard, dried thyme, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Add 2 cups of water to the skillet and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the grains are tender and the liquid has been absorbed.
Stir in the chopped fresh parsley and dill.
Serve hot, garnished with crushed red pepper flakes if desired.
Let the pilaf rest for 5 minutes before serving to allow the grains to fluff up and the flavors to meld together.
Adjust the seasoning as needed with salt and pepper.
Experiment with different spices and herbs to give the pilaf your own unique flavor.
Try adding cooked vegetables or beans to make the pilaf more substantial and nutritious.
Consider serving the pilaf with a dollop of sour cream or a sprinkle of chopped fresh herbs for added flavor and texture.
